back: A logical connective is an operator used to combine two or more propositions to form a more complex compound proposition. The truth value of the compound proposition depends on the truth values of the component propositions and the connective used.

front: What is the symbol and truth table definition for the Negation (NOT) connective?
back:
Symbol: ¬p or ~p
Truth Table:
p | ¬p
T | F
F | T
It reverses the truth value of the proposition.

front: What is the Conjunction (AND) connective and its truth table?
back:
Symbol: p ∧ q
Means 'p and q'. The conjunction is true ONLY when both p and q are true.
Truth Table:
p | q | p ∧ q
T | T | T
T | F | F
F | T | F
F | F | F

front: What is the Disjunction (OR) connective and its truth table?
back:
Symbol: p ∨ q
Means 'p or q'. In the inclusive OR used in logic, the disjunction is true if AT LEAST ONE of p or q is true.
Truth Table:
p | q | p ∨ q
T | T | T
T | F | T
F | T | T
F | F | F

front: What is the truth table for the Conditional (Implication) connective p → q?
back:
The statement p → q is false only when the antecedent p is true and the consequent q is false. It is true in all other cases.
Truth Table:
p | q | p → q
T | T | T
T | F | F
F | T | T
F | F | T
